name: Accompany Capital
description_short: Accompany Capital is a New York-based CDFI providing affordable small business loans, financial training, and capacity-building services to underserved entrepreneurs, refugees, and women business owners for over 20 years.
description_long:
Accompany Capital is a mission-driven lending organization headquartered in Manhattan with a second office in Queens that has spent over two decades serving New York's underserved entrepreneurial communities. The organization was founded with a specific focus on supporting new Americans, refugees with legal status, and women entrepreneurs who face barriers to traditional financing and business support. Their deep expertise in serving immigrant and refugee populations reflects a commitment to understanding the unique challenges these groups face when starting businesses in a new country while navigating credit systems and regulatory requirements.

Accompany Capital offers a range of integrated financial and non-financial services. Their core product is affordable small business loans with quick approval processes, manageable terms, and no hidden fees. Beyond lending, they provide comprehensive workshops and training in business management, financial literacy, credit building, marketing, and technology. For eligible clients, they offer a HomeFirst Down Payment Assistance Program to support first-time homeownership. Their service model emphasizes hands-on partnership and personalized guidance rather than transactional lending alone.

What distinguishes Accompany Capital is their explicit focus on underserved populations combined with 20+ years of specialized experience. They don't simply offer loans; they position themselves as partners in their clients' entrepreneurial journeys, providing education and training alongside capital. Their organization actively seeks funding partners, works with government and nonprofit organizations, and accepts donations to sustain their mission. They publish impact metrics and success stories, and recently launched the Leonid Ostrovsky Lending Fellowship to develop emerging talent in the lending space.

As a mission-driven CDFI rather than a commercial lender, Accompany Capital's primary limitation is geographic scope—they serve only the New York area. Their loan products appear designed for micro and small businesses rather than larger enterprises. While their website emphasizes affordability and quick approval, specific APR ranges, loan amounts, and repayment terms are not disclosed publicly, requiring applicants to contact the organization directly for detailed product information.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Over 20 years of specialized experience lending to underserved micro and small business owners with demonstrated understanding of immigrant and refugee entrepreneurship challenges
Integrated approach combining affordable loans with free workshops, business training, financial literacy education, and technology training
Specific programs tailored to distinct populations: New Americans, refugees with legal status, and women entrepreneurs with targeted support addressing each group's unique challenges
HomeFirst Down Payment Assistance Program providing home financing support and education for first-time homebuyers alongside business lending
No hidden fees on loans with quick approval processes and manageable terms designed for borrowers who may lack traditional credit history
Transparent about funding model and mission—actively recruits funders, partners, and donors; publishes impact data and success stories
Bilingual and culturally competent service delivery implicit in mission focus on new Americans and refugees
cons: Geographic limitation: serves only New York area (Manhattan and Queens offices documented), not available nationwide
Loan products designed for micro and small businesses; may not accommodate larger or growth-stage enterprises
Critical loan terms not disclosed on website—specific APR ranges, loan amount limits, and repayment periods require direct contact; no online rate calculator or pre-qualification tool
As a CDFI with mission focus, lending may be more restrictive than commercial lenders; business type eligibility requirements not publicly specified
Limited information about underwriting timeline despite claims of 'quick approval process'
best_for: Immigrant and refugee entrepreneurs in New York seeking affordable business loans with cultural competency and business training support
Women small business owners in New York who want targeted financing and networking with other female entrepreneurs
First-time homebuyers in the New York area, particularly those with limited down payment savings or non-traditional credit histories
diagnosis: Accompany Capital is best for New York-based immigrant, refugee, and women entrepreneurs seeking affordable small business loans paired with comprehensive business training and financial education in a culturally competent environment. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to the New York area and lack of publicly disclosed loan terms, requiring direct application to understand specific APR, loan amounts, and eligibility criteria.
services: Affordable small business loans with quick approval and no hidden fees
Business training and workshops covering operations, strategy, and growth
Financial literacy and credit building education
HomeFirst Down Payment Assistance Program for first-time homebuyers
Home buying navigation workshops and counseling
Networking and peer support community for women entrepreneurs
Personal financial management counseling for refugees and asylees
Credit building programs tailored to new Americans
Access to capital matching services and lending partnerships
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: New York CDFI providing small business loans, financial training, and capacity-building to underserved entrepreneurs for over 20 years.
